what_is_going_wrong: HB Logistics margin fell to 4.8% in March from 8.3% in February because temporary labor and fuel costs ran above plan.
Airport Expansion is $400K over budget, with electrical and materials spend driving the overrun.
New Development Site A is $400K over budget and is blocking revenue recognition.
Month-end close is blocked by missing Logistics invoices and a late Development revenue recognition task.
what_to_focus_on: Get the signed Site A change order into the close packet.
Contain Airport Expansion electrical and materials overrun.
Consolidate duplicated vendor contracts across entities.

demo_script: Open Hall Boys Operating System from the environment list and land on Executive.
Call out consolidated March revenue of $12.5M, weighted margin of 12.0%, and two at-risk projects.
Click Airport Expansion to show budget vs actual, vendor overrun, linked contract, and close task.
Jump to Vendors and show Apex Electrical and Prime Staffing duplicated across entities with overspend vs contract.
Go to Documents, open the Apex contract, and point out the $250K due in 30 days plus the auto-renewal clause.
Ask Winston: What should I focus on today? and confirm the answer mentions Logistics close blockers, Site A revenue recognition, and the two red projects.
improvements: Swap the seeded fixture for live finance, task, and vendor joins once the operator entities are modeled in canonical tables.
Add owner-level drilldowns for project and close accountability.
Persist uploaded operator documents back into project and vendor drilldowns automatically.
Add contract-versus-actual variance alerts that publish directly into the close surface.

ordinance_documents.title: Dallas PD-830 Bishop Arts Area Plan

ordinance_rules.title: Residential parking minimum — T6 urban core
ordinance_rules.summary: Minimum 1.5 parking spaces per DU within T6 transect zones, up from 1.0.

ordinance_rules.title: Residential side setback — T6
ordinance_rules.summary: 15 ft minimum side setback on lots < 1.5 acres in T6 transect zones.

ordinance_rules.title: T6 maximum height
ordinance_rules.summary: 240 ft height limit in T6-24 transect zones.

ordinance_rules.title: Compatibility standards — CS-MU adjacency
ordinance_rules.summary: Stepped height limits within 200 ft of SF-3 adjacency; applies to Riverside corridor.

ordinance_rules.title: Impervious cover + drainage
ordinance_rules.summary: Max 65% impervious cover with TPDES drainage plan required on parcels > 1 acre.

ordinance_rules.title: PD-830 front setback
ordinance_rules.summary: Build-to zone between 5 ft and 15 ft on N Bishop frontage.

rule_change_events.summary: Miami-Dade raised T6 residential parking minimums from 1.0 to 1.5 spaces per DU. Two Hall Boys sites under diligence are impacted.

secondary_effects: May require variance filings adding 90+ days

rule_change_events.summary: Austin adopted compatibility step-down standards on CS-MU adjacency. Austin Riverside site requires envelope redesign.

secondary_effects: Density reduction may push pro-forma below hurdle
Risks losing site control before reapproval

rule_change_events.summary: Dallas raised PD-830 permit review fees and added two plan review rounds. Airport Expansion electrical rev 3 is now 18 days over median review time.

feasibility_assessments.summary: Likely requires setback variance. Comparable T6 approvals in Miami-Dade took 4–6 months longer than the typical cycle.

constraints.note: 15 ft side setback violated by current envelope.
New parking minimum adds ~$18K/unit at 1.5 spaces/DU.

recommended_actions: Apply for side-setback variance before site control expires.
Re-run financial scenarios with 1.5 spaces/DU parking ratio.

feasibility_assessments.summary: Parking ratio change is the primary driver. No setback variance required.

constraints.note: New minimum pushes parking count by 48 stalls on current design.

recommended_actions: Evaluate mechanical stackers vs surface lot redesign.
Review with lender — parking cost drives ~$4M of basis.

feasibility_assessments.summary: New compatibility standards force envelope step-down adjacent to SF-3. Density drops from 320 to 240 DU. Timeline extends by 90–120 days.

constraints.note: Impervious cover exceeds 65% on current site plan.

recommended_actions: Re-stack massing with compatibility envelope before further investment.
Commission drainage study ahead of submittal to avoid comment loops.

feasibility_assessments.summary: Approved. Execution risk only — electrical scope driving overrun.
recommended_actions: Expedite electrical rev 3 review with Dallas permit office.

feasibility_assessments.summary: Clean entitlement path; timeline tracks with Denver median.
recommended_actions: Release site to construction staffing for Q3 start.

feasibility_assessments.summary: PD-830 build-to zone constrains street frontage. Similar Bishop Arts projects required 3 review rounds on average.

constraints.note: Current scheme violates 5–15 ft build-to zone.

recommended_actions: Revise ground floor massing to meet PD-830 build-to.
Pre-brief PD-830 review committee before formal submittal.

comparable_projects.name: Edgewater 84
comparable_projects.notes: Setback variance granted after 4 months of appeals.

comparable_projects.name: Wynwood Tower
comparable_projects.notes: Mechanical stackers used to meet parking minimums.

comparable_projects.name: East Riverside Plaza
comparable_projects.notes: Denied on compatibility grounds; resubmitted 2 years later.

comparable_projects.name: DFW Cargo Phase 2
comparable_projects.notes: Comparable scope and schedule to Airport Expansion.

comparable_projects.name: Commons Park Residences
comparable_projects.notes: Clean approval; Denver Union Station tracks similarly.

comparable_projects.name: Bishop Block 3
comparable_projects.notes: Required 3 review rounds to pass PD-830 build-to.

weekly_summary.critical_path: Permitting in Dallas and Austin is the bottleneck driving roughly 80% of current delay risk.
weekly_summary.headline: Defensive posture — permitting delays in Dallas and Austin are the critical path driving ~$410K of risk. Immediate intervention required to prevent spill into next quarter.
key_shifts: Miami-Dade raised T6 residential parking minimums — 2 active sites now require variance or redesign.
Airport Expansion crossed the 18-day permit-over-median threshold this week.
Apex Electrical is now on 42% of active jobs — concentration risk flagged.
recommended_actions: Call Dallas permit office on Airport Expansion Monday.
Review Brickell 18 + Wynwood 42 for parking variance before further spend.
Unblock closeout on New Development Site A — missing lien waivers are holding $640K.
